title: signal SIGSEGV: invalid address in final block
body:
Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

We see this failure failure when we grew the number of rows in a similar array/function from 2048 to 8192.  Not understanding the root-cause, I had to put a much larger temp array in the shrunk-down example.

Shrunk-down example ...

```systemverilog
module static_array_in_func (
    input bit reset,
    input bit clock
);
      
      typedef bit [127:0] row_t;
      
      function automatic void foo ();
        row_t ary [2**19];  // passes with 2**18 rows
        $writememh("somefile", ary, 0);
      endfunction: foo

      initial begin
        $finish();
      end

      final begin
        foo();
      end
      
endmodule: static_array_in_func
```
(this code works here ... https://www.edaplayground.com/x/3x33)

with that code and some boilerplate C-main code, 
compiled with ...
```sh
$ verilator --cc --exe -sv --assert --vpi --trace --top-module static_array_in_func ./testcase.sv verilator-main.cpp -CFLAGS "-std=c++14"
```

I get ...
```
$ lldb -- obj_dir/Vstatic_array_in_func +random_seed=100
(lldb) target create "obj_dir/Vstatic_array_in_func"
Current executable set to 'obj_dir/Vstatic_array_in_func' (x86_64).
(lldb) settings set -- target.run-args  "+random_seed=100"
(lldb) run
Process 17891 launched: '$DIR/obj_dir/Vstatic_array_in_func' (x86_64)
INFO(../verilator-main.cpp): Random seed is 100
- testcase.sv:15: Verilog $finish
Process 17891 stopped
* thread #1, name = 'Vstatic_array_i', stop reason = signal SIGSEGV: invalid address (fault address: 0x7fffff7fd458)
    frame #0: 0x0000000000435df8 Vstatic_array_in_func`Vstatic_array_in_func::_final_TOP(vlSymsp=<unavailable>) at Vstatic_array_in_func.cpp:90
   87       } while (VL_UNLIKELY(__Vchange));
   88   }
   89  
-> 90   void Vstatic_array_in_func::_final_TOP(Vstatic_array_in_func__Syms* __restrict vlSymsp) {
   91       VL_DEBUG_IF(VL_DBG_MSGF("+    Vstatic_array_in_func::_final_TOP\n"); );
   92       // Variables
   93       WData/*127:0*/ __Vtask_static_array_in_func__DOT__foo__0__ary[524288][4];
(lldb) bt
* thread #1, name = 'Vstatic_array_i', stop reason = signal SIGSEGV: invalid address (fault address: 0x7fffff7fd458)
  * frame #0: 0x0000000000435df8 Vstatic_array_in_func`Vstatic_array_in_func::_final_TOP(vlSymsp=<unavailable>) at Vstatic_array_in_func.cpp:90
    frame #1: 0x0000000000435f8c Vstatic_array_in_func`Vstatic_array_in_func::final(this=0x000000000066b3c0) at Vstatic_array_in_func.cpp:127
    frame #2: 0x0000000000404cd0 Vstatic_array_in_func`main(argc=2, argv=0x00007fffffffd968, env=0x00007fffffffd980) at verilator-main.cpp:160
    frame #3: 0x00007ffff7495830 libc.so.6`__libc_start_main(main=(Vstatic_array_in_func`main at verilator-main.cpp:48), argc=2, argv=0x00007fffffffd968, init=<unavailable>, fini=<unavailable>, rtld_fini=<unavailable>, stack_end=0x00007fffffffd958) at libc-start.c:291
    frame #4: 0x0000000000404379 Vstatic_array_in_func`_start + 41
(lldb) quit
```


What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

I tested this on 4.028 and 4.034.  No, I have not tried this on `master`.

Would you be willing to try to fix Verilator yourself with assistance?

I am not fluent in C/C++, so I am not comfortable with trying to fix this at this time.
